source: Product reset corpus and baseline method taxonomy

summary.en: Each band emits one discretized magnitude token; a spectrum becomes an ordered phrase.
summary.es: Cada banda emite un token de magnitud discretizada; un espectro se vuelve una frase ordenada.

alphabet_definition.en: Finite set of magnitude bins such as q00, q01, ..., q15.
alphabet_definition.es: Conjunto finito de bins de magnitud como q00, q01, ..., q15.

word_definition.en: One quantized magnitude level emitted by one band position.
word_definition.es: Un nivel de magnitud cuantizado emitido por una posicion de banda.

document_definition.en: One spectrum, ordered by band position; region documents aggregate multiple phrases only when order metadata is retained.
document_definition.es: Un espectro ordenado por posicion de banda; documentos de region agregan varias frases solo si se conserva la metadata de orden.

risks.en: Wavelength identity is weak unless position or band-group metadata is preserved.
risks.es: La identidad de longitud de onda es debil salvo que se preserve posicion o grupo de bandas.

title.es: Alfabeto de bandas, frecuencia de magnitud

summary.en: Each band is a word; its magnitude becomes a count or weight.
summary.es: Cada banda es una palabra; su magnitud se vuelve conteo o peso.

alphabet_definition.en: Band or wavelength identifiers such as b042 or 2200nm.
alphabet_definition.es: Identificadores de banda o longitud de onda como b042 o 2200nm.

word_definition.en: One band token repeated or weighted by transformed magnitude.
word_definition.es: Un token de banda repetido o ponderado por magnitud transformada.

document_definition.en: One spectrum or region represented as a band-frequency histogram.
document_definition.es: Un espectro o region representado como histograma de frecuencias por banda.

risks.en: High albedo or high-response bands can dominate unless normalized.
risks.es: Bandas de alto albedo o respuesta alta pueden dominar salvo que se normalicen.

summary.en: Each word joins band or wavelength group with a quantized magnitude.
summary.es: Cada palabra une banda o grupo de longitud de onda con magnitud cuantizada.

alphabet_definition.en: Joint band and magnitude tokens such as b042_q07, 2200nm_q03, or swir2_abs_high.
alphabet_definition.es: Tokens conjuntos de banda y magnitud como b042_q07, 2200nm_q03 o swir2_abs_high.

word_definition.en: One spectral event combining location and response level.
word_definition.es: Un evento espectral que combina ubicacion y nivel de respuesta.

document_definition.en: One spectrum, patch, ROI, class, superpixel, or sample support encoded as a bag of spectral events.
document_definition.es: Un espectro, parche, ROI, clase, superpixel o soporte de muestra codificado como bolsa de eventos espectrales.

risks.en: Vocabulary grows quickly and can become too sparse.
risks.es: El vocabulario crece rapido y puede volverse demasiado disperso.

summary.en: A patch, ROI, class, SLIC superpixel, or measurement support aggregates words from multiple spectra.
summary.es: Un parche, ROI, clase, superpixel SLIC o soporte de medicion agrega palabras de multiples espectros.

alphabet_definition.en: Any selected spectral alphabet, aggregated over a spatial or sample support.
alphabet_definition.es: Cualquier alfabeto espectral seleccionado, agregado sobre un soporte espacial o de muestra.

word_definition.en: One spectral event emitted by one spectrum inside the region.
word_definition.es: Un evento espectral emitido por un espectro dentro de la region.

document_definition.en: One patch, ROI, label class, superpixel, or sample support.
document_definition.es: Un parche, ROI, clase etiquetada, superpixel o soporte de muestra.

risks.en: Document boundaries define topic semantics and must be compared.
risks.es: Los limites del documento definen la semantica del topico y deben compararse.

summary.en: Words encode slopes, curvature, continuum-removed absorption depth, center, width, and asymmetry.
summary.es: Las palabras codifican pendientes, curvatura, profundidad de absorcion con continuo removido, centro, ancho y asimetria.

alphabet_definition.en: Spectral-shape and absorption-feature bins tied to calibrated wavelength regions.
alphabet_definition.es: Bins de forma espectral y rasgos de absorcion ligados a regiones de longitud de onda calibradas.

word_definition.en: One shape or absorption event, not a mineral label.
word_definition.es: Un evento de forma o absorcion, no una etiqueta mineral.

document_definition.en: One spectrum or region with reliable wavelength metadata and bad-band handling.
document_definition.es: Un espectro o region con metadata confiable de longitud de onda y manejo de bandas malas.

risks.en: Approximate wavelength axes are not enough for mineral/clay absorption claims.
risks.es: Ejes aproximados de longitud de onda no bastan para afirmaciones de absorcion mineral/arcilla.

summary.en: Documents link sample, region, and spectrum levels for measured sample supports.
summary.es: Los documentos enlazan niveles de muestra, region y espectro para soportes medidos.

alphabet_definition.en: Any selected spectral alphabet plus hierarchy identifiers.
alphabet_definition.es: Cualquier alfabeto espectral seleccionado mas identificadores jerarquicos.

word_definition.en: One spectral or region event associated with a sample hierarchy.
word_definition.es: Un evento espectral o regional asociado a una jerarquia de muestra.

document_definition.en: Sample -> region -> spectrum hierarchy where top-level samples have response variables.
document_definition.es: Jerarquia muestra -> region -> espectro donde las muestras superiores tienen variables de respuesta.

risks.en: Should wait until a curated Family D subset exists.
risks.es: Debe esperar hasta que exista un subconjunto curado de Familia D.

baselines.name: Raw spectra model
baselines.purpose: Minimum supervised classifier or regressor baseline.

baselines.name: PCA feature model
baselines.feature_space: principal components from spectra or region aggregates

baselines.name: KMeans clustering
baselines.feature_space: spectra, topic mixtures, or region features

baselines.name: Gaussian mixture clustering
baselines.purpose: Soft clustering baseline with probabilistic assignments.
baselines.feature_space: spectra, topic mixtures, or region features
baselines.caveat: Gaussian assumptions may fail for spectral mixtures.

baselines.name: Hierarchical clustering
baselines.feature_space: spectra, topic mixtures, or region features
baselines.caveat: Can be expensive on many pixels.

baselines.name: SLIC superpixels
baselines.purpose: Spatial boundary baseline and region-document generator.
baselines.caveat: Standard SLIC must be adapted carefully for hyperspectral cubes.

baselines.name: Spectral angle / reference matching
baselines.caveat: A close reference is not a material identification claim.

baselines.name: NMF / unmixing baseline
baselines.caveat: Requires component count and nonnegativity assumptions.

baselines.name: Topic-mixture model
baselines.caveat: Only valid with documented labels or measurements.

baselines.name: Topic-routed model
baselines.purpose: Specialist classifier or regressor selected by dominant topic or topic group.
baselines.feature_space: topic mixture gates plus local spectral or topic features
baselines.caveat: Requires enough samples per topic group.

validation_blocks.title: Corpus integrity
validation_blocks.question: Can the generated corpus be inspected and reproduced?

validation_blocks.title: Topic stability
validation_blocks.question: Do topics remain comparable across random seeds and topic counts?

validation_blocks.title: Quantization sensitivity
validation_blocks.question: Does the interpretation survive bin-count and binning-policy changes?

validation_blocks.title: Document-definition sensitivity
validation_blocks.question: How do pixel, patch, ROI, superpixel, and sample documents change the result?

validation_blocks.title: Label or measurement association
validation_blocks.question: Do topic mixtures explain labels or response measurements better than baselines?

validation_blocks.title: Spectral-library alignment
validation_blocks.question: Do topic or region profiles align with known reference spectra?
